reason: Response A has the more direct, analytical structure characteristic of how I typically approach open-ended aesthetic questions — breaking the topic into named components (proportion, rhythm, transparency, surprise) with concrete examples like Nabokov and Didion, which reflects my tendency to ground abstract observations in specific references. Response B is beautifully written but feels more performatively literary, with the recursive self-referential opening ("a sentence I keep returning to") and heavy reliance on aphoristic fragments that read more like an imitation of a particular writerly style than my natural expository voice. That said, both responses are strong and this distinction is subtle.
